description: python-ecdsa core: GF(p) modular arithmetic + elliptic-curve group law over GF(p) in affine coordinates. Translated from python-ecdsa (ecdsa/numbertheory.py + ecdsa/ellipticcurve.py). The headline obligation is inverseMod (python-ecdsa's inverse_mod): existence-and-uniqueness of the modular multiplicative inverse, stated as the frozen-op-anchored equation (a*inv)%m=1 together with the mandatory range clause 0<inv<m that pins the UNIQUE residue; the proof runs the genuine extended-Euclid Bézout identity (egcd Bézout invariant + gcd-link), so there is no copyable oracle and no self-referential round-trip. The elliptic-curve layer pins the affine group law to the frozen slope arithmetic: containsPoint is an iff against the frozen Weierstrass residue y^2 = x^3 + a*x + b (mod p); pointAdd and pointDouble are pinned to their explicit coordinate formulas (chord slope lambda=(y2-y1)*inv(x2-x1) and tangent slope lambda=(3x^2+a)*inv(2y), with x3=lambda^2-... and y3=lambda*(x-x3)-y) over the frozen inverseMod. The doubling branch is closed by its own formula spec, by the 2y=0 -> infinity vertical-tangent law, by the addition-delegates-to-doubling spec (pointAdd P P = pointDouble P), and by the scalarMult recurrence (k+1)*P = k*P + P that routes the scalar ladder through the adder and doubler. On-curve closure and group correctness are anchored by frozen golden vectors on y^2 = x^3 + 2x + 2 (mod 17) with generator (5,1): 2G=(6,3), 3G=(10,6), 4G=(3,1), -G, and scalarMult agree with the standard values and stay on-curve. The square-root obligation (sqrtModPrime squares-back) is anchored by concrete primality/Legendre-symbol vectors (isOddPrime, jacobi) plus a frozen primality iff, so it is never agent-vacuous. Scope: the benchmark models the GF(p) inverse, the on-curve characterization, the coordinate formulas, on-curve closure (anchored), and the structural identity/negation/scalar-recurrence laws — NOT signature gen/verify, full Tonelli-Shanks, or group ASSOCIATIVITY (mathlib-scale). Everything is discrete Nat/Int; no Float. inverseMod is total/terminating via a fuel-bounded extended Euclid.
